etymology.description: 大(大) (dà, "big; great") 安(安) (ān, "peace; safe") 區(區) (qū, "area; district")
etymology.interpretation: 大安(大安) (dà'ān, "great peace") combines 'great' and 'peace', reflecting an ideal or desirable state of tranquility and well-being, often used as a name for places aiming to convey these qualities. 區(區) (qū, "area; district") denotes a division or administrative region, hence 大安區(大安區) (Dà'ān Qū) specifically refers to the Da’an District in Taipei, suggesting it as an area of large tranquility or prominence.

examples.chinese: 大安区的公园很美丽(大安區的公園很美麗)。
examples.pinyin: Dà’ān Qū de gōngyuán hěn měilì.
examples.english: The parks in Da’an District are beautiful.

examples.chinese: 我住在大安区(我住在大安區)，每天都在附近跑步(每天都在附近跑步)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zhù zài Dà’ān Qū, měitiān dōu zài fùjìn pǎobù.
examples.english: I live in Da’an District and run nearby every day.

examples.chinese: 大安区有许多不错的餐厅(大安區有許多不錯的餐廳)。
examples.pinyin: Dà’ān Qū yǒu xǔduō bùcuò de cāntīng.
examples.english: Da’an District has many great restaurants.
